---
title: "Home"
---
<div class="homepage-hero">
<div class="homepage-copy">
<p class="homepage-kicker">Econometrics | Causal Inference | Applied Microeconomics</p>
<p class="homepage-lead">I am an Assistant Professor of Economics at the University of Alabama's Culverhouse College of Business.</p>
<p class="homepage-summary">My research is in econometrics, with interests in causal inference, estimation, inference, hypothesis testing, and uniform inference. I develop methods that help researchers make more credible empirical claims, especially in settings with complex data, weak identifying assumptions, and challenging inference problems. My work combines theory with applied questions in economics.</p>
</div>
<img src="assets/images/Emmanuel_Selorm_Tsyawo2018.jpg" alt="Portrait of Emmanuel S. Tsyawo" class="homepage-photo" />
</div>

<div class="homepage-grid">
<section class="homepage-card">
<h3>Quick links</h3>
<div class="quick-links">
<a class="quick-link" href="research.html">Research</a>
<a class="quick-link" href="files/cv/Tsyawo_CV.pdf">Curriculum Vitae</a>
<a class="quick-link" href="code.html">Code</a>
</div>
</section>

<section class="homepage-card contact-block" id="contact">
<h3>Contact</h3>
<p><strong>Emmanuel S. Tsyawo</strong><br />
Assistant Professor of Economics<br />
Culverhouse College of Business, University of Alabama</p>
<p>Email: estsyawo [at] gmail.com<br />
<a href="https://github.com/estsyawo">GitHub</a></p>
</section>
</div>

<section class="homepage-card">
<h3>Recent updates</h3>
<ul class="news-list">
<li>Forthcoming publication: <a href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2408.13047">Difference-in-differences with as few as two cross-sectional units – A new perspective to the democracy-growth debate</a>, with Gilles Koumou, <em>Econometric Reviews</em>.</li>
<li>New working paper: <a href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.12284">A Grid-Rate Condition for Valid Uniform Inference</a> (2026).</li>
<li>New R package: <a href="https://github.com/estsyawo/icmkit">icmkit</a> provides tools for Integrated Conditional Moment (ICM) estimation and specification testing in linear and nonlinear regression settings.</li>
<li>Recent publication: <a href="https://www.cambridge.org/core/journals/econometric-theory/article/consistent-icmbased-chi2-specification-test/DF98A874F40AA24D1414C62C4F2357C9">A Consistent ICM-based χ² Specification Test</a>, with Feiyu Jiang, <em>Econometric Theory</em> (2026).</li>
<li>Scheduled presentation: <a href="https://www.econometricsociety.org/regional-activities/schedule/2026/06/10/2026-Africa-Meeting-Cairo-Egypt">Africa Meeting of the Econometric Society (AFES)</a>, Cairo, Egypt (June 2026).</li>
</ul>
</section>

<!--

* [Southern Economic Association 95th Annual Meeting](https://southerneconomic.org/conference/call-for-papers/), Tampa, Florida, (November 22-24)

* [2025 World Congress of the Econometric Society (ESWC 2025)](https://www.eswc2025.org/), Seoul, Korea, (August 18-22, 2025)

* [The 19th World Conference of the Spatial Econometrics Association (SEA 2025)](https://sites.google.com/site/jefconference/), Rabat, Morocco, (May 5-6 2025)

* The 29th International Panel Data Conference (IPDC), Orléans, France (July 2024)

* Bernoulli-IMS 11th World Congress in Probability and Statistics, Bochum, Germany (August 2024)

* European Summer Meeting of the Econometric Society, Rotterdam, Netherlands (August, 2024)

* The 18th International Joint Conference on Computational and Financial Econometrics (CFE) and Computational and Methodological Statistics (CMStatistics), CFE-CMStatistics 2024, London (14-16 December 2024)

-->
